titles.en: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes

text: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْفِرَ اَلْبِئْرَ فِي دَارِهِ أَوْ فِي أَرْضِهِ فَقَالَ "أَمَّا مَا حَفَرَ فِي مِلْكِهِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ ضَمَانٌ وَ أَمَّا مَا حَفَرَ فِي اَلطَّرِيقِ أَوْ فِي غَيْرِ مِلْكِهِ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ لِمَا يَسْقُطُ فِيهَا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5341 - Zurah and Uthman ibn Isa narrated from Samaah, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who digs a well in his house or on his land.
Imam (as) said: "If he digs it within his own property, there is no liability upon him. But if he digs it in a public road or in a place that does not belong to him, then he is liable for anything that falls into it."

text: رِوَايَةِ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلْجُسُورِ أَ يَضْمَنُ أَهْلُهَا شَيْئاً قَالَ "لاَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.5342 - In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman from one of our companions, from Abu Abdullah (as), he was asked about bridges and whether their owners are liable for anything. Imam (as) replied: "No."

text: رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ أَخْرَجَ مِيزَاباً أَوْ كَنِيفاً أَوْ وَتَدَ وَتِداً أَوْ أَوْثَقَ دَابَّةً أَوْ حَفَرَ بِئْراً فِي طَرِيقِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ فَأَصَابَ شَيْئاً فَعَطِبَ فَهُوَ لَهُ ضَامِنٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5343 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Whoever extends a water spout, a drain, drives a peg, ties an animal, or digs a well in the path of Muslims and it causes harm or damage to anything, then he is liable for it."

parts.text: رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ هِلاَلٍ

text: : "كَانَ مِنْ قَضَاءِ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "أَنَّ اَلْمَعْدِنَ جُبَارٌ وَ اَلْبِئْرَ جُبَارٌ وَ اَلْعَجْمَاءَ جُبَارٌ" ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5344 - Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Hilal narrated from Uqbah ibn Khalid, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Among the rulings of the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) was:
'There is no liability (Jubar) for injuries caused by a mine, a well, or an animal.'"

text: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ غُلاَمٍ دَخَلَ دَارَ قَوْمٍ يَلْعَبُ فَوَقَعَ فِي بِئْرِهِمْ أَ يَضْمَنُونَ قَالَ "لَيْسَ يَضْمَنُونَ وَ إِنْ كَانُوا مُتَّهَمِينَ ضَمِنُوا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5345 - Wuhayb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a boy who entered the house of some people to play and fell into their well. Are they liable?
Imam (as) said: "They are not liable, but if they are accused (of negligence or wrongdoing), then they are liable."

text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ أَضَرَّ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ طَرِيقِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ فَهُوَ لَهُ ضَامِنٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5346 - Al-Husayn ibn Said narrated from Ali ibn Al-Numan, who narrated from Abu Al-Sabbah Al-Kinani, who narrates that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"Whoever causes harm to any part of the path of the Muslims is liable for it."

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ
اَلشَّيْءِ يُوضَعُ عَلَى اَلطَّرِيقِ فَتَمُرُّ بِهِ اَلدَّابَّةُ فَتَنْفِرُ بِصَاحِبِهَا فَتَعْقِرُهُ

text: "كُلُّ شَيْءٍ يُضِرُّ بِطَرِيقِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ فَصَاحِبُهُ ضَامِنٌ لِمَا يُصِيبُهُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5347 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about an object placed on the road, and an animal passes by it, becomes startled, and injures its rider.
Imam (as) said: "Anything that causes harm on the path of the Muslims, its owner is liable for whatever results from it."
